content.problemStatement:
**This problem has the same setup as the hard version. The difference is here you only need the value of the whole string, while in the hard version you need the sum over all substrings.**

You are the owner of a shop with NNN different unique items to sell, numbered 1,2,…,N1, 2, \ldots, N1,2,…,N. The items have been numbered such that item iii is always larger (in size) than item i−1i - 1i−1. The cost of the iii-th item is CiC_iCi​. The cost of an item **does not have** any correlation with the size.

There is a display zone in your shop, and customers are only able to see items that are on the display zone. The rules are as follows:


At the start of each day, you can choose to add some items to the display zone.
Once an item has been placed on the display zone, **it cannot be removed** until a customer buys it.
When a customer comes, he must see at least 222 different items on the display zone, to ensure that he has some freedom of choice.
Once a customer buys an item, it cannot be bought by another customer, and it cannot be kept on the display zone.
K(1≤K<N)K (1 \le K < N)K(1≤K<N) customers will come to your shop, one by one, on days 111, 222, …\ldots…, KKK respectively. On the iii-th day, the customer has a preference of AiA_iAi​, where Ai=0A_i = 0Ai​=0 means that he will buy the smallest item (which is present on the display zone), and Ai=1A_i = 1Ai​=1 means that he will buy the largest item (which is present on the display zone). Note that AAA is a binary string.

You want to maximize the total revenue that you get. Your revenue is the sum of CiC_iCi​ over all sold items.

Let f(A)f(A)f(A) denote the maximum possible revenue corresponding to the binary string AAA of length KKK (representing the preferences of the customers).

You are given NNN, the cost of all items CiC_iCi​ and a binary string AAA of length KKK.

Find f(A)f(A)f(A) modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains 222 integers NNN and KKK - the number of items, and the number of customers.
The second line contains NNN integers - C1,C2,…,CNC_1, C_2, \ldots, C_NC1​,C2​,…,CN​.
The third line contains a binary string AAA of length KKK.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line f(A)f(A)f(A) modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1: We are forced to keep both the 222 items on display zone for the first and only customer (due to the constraint of at least 222 items on display). The customer buys the larger item, which is item 222, and that gives us a revenue of 101010.
Test Case 2: For the first customer, we keep all the items 111, 222 and 333 on display. He buys the largest item, i.e. item 333. Then, the second customer comes and buys item numbered 222.
Test Case 4: Do not forget to print your answer modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.
